lesson.menu_desc: Past continuous + past simple (while / when) -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 3 -- Telling Stories -- Past Continuous + Past Simple (while / when)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Sandra Hayasaki | Aula 3 -- Telling Stories

listenings.text: Let me tell you about something that happened to me last year. I was driving home from work on a normal Friday evening. The traffic was slow, and I was thinking about dinner. Suddenly, the car in front of me stopped. While I was braking, I noticed a small dog running across the road. I stopped just in time. The dog was scared, so I got out and picked it up. It had no collar. Meanwhile, a woman was running toward me, waving her arms. She was crying. It was her dog. At that moment, I realized how lucky we both were. She thanked me again and again. It was a completely unexpected end to an ordinary day.

I want to tell you about a trip I took a few years ago. My family and I were visiting a small town in the mountains. One afternoon, we were walking through the old market. While we were looking at some handmade bowls, I noticed an old woman sitting in the corner. She was painting. Her work was beautiful. Suddenly, she looked up and smiled at me. She said something in a language I did not understand, but the moment felt warm and kind. Meanwhile, my husband was taking photos of everything. At that moment, I realized that the best part of travel is not the famous places. It is the unexpected people you meet along the way.

extra_audio.text: First, set the scene: say what was happening. Then introduce a sudden action with suddenly or when. Next, describe what you noticed at that moment. After that, explain what happened next. Finally, say how you felt about the whole experience.
